ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for this Vietnamese-Singaporean composite dish. A00LC (pork offal) is used as the closest defensible anchor for the dominant protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept maps to this specific composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of stir-fried pickled cabbage with pork offal is approximately 200 g, representing one plate as served in a Singaporean hawker centre or Vietnamese home meal.

nutrient_source.title: Stir Fried Cabbage Pickles With Pork Organ (Cai Chua Xao Long)

nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for this dish (cr_id G00010535). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from HPB with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins are not reported in the HPB entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Carbohydrate content is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g by HPB. This dish is a protein- and fat-dominant preparation with negligible carbohydrates. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates; the value 0 is used as a numeric sentinel consistent with the template requirement for a numeric field, not as a measured GI value. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. No GI testing of this dish is recorded in the University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium content at 1050 mg per 100 g, derived from both pickled cabbage and fish sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this dish; sodium at 1050 mg/100 g far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold, and the dual sodium load from pickled cabbage and fish sauce cannot be meaningfully reduced by rinsing alone.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sodium content worsening insulin resistance, combined with high cholesterol and saturated fat from pork offal impairing insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid this dish; the 1050 mg/100 g sodium load promotes insulin resistance and fluid retention, and the high cholesterol (89 mg/100 g) from pork offal further impairs metabolic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1050 mg/100 g) and high cholesterol (89 mg/100 g) from pork offal, with no dietary fibre or potassium offset
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this dish; the combination of very high sodium, high dietary cholesterol from pork organs, and negligible potassium (47 mg/100 g) and fibre creates an unfavourable cardiovascular risk profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High arachidonic acid and saturated fat from pork offal amplifying inflammatory pathways, compounded by high sodium worsening tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish; pork offal is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6) which amplifies inflammatory cytokines, and the high sodium content from pickled cabbage and fish sauce exacerbates joint swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cai Chua Xao Long (酸菜炒猪杂) is a Vietnamese-origin stir-fried dish widely available in Singaporean hawker centres, consisting of pickled mustard cabbage (cai chua) stir-fried with mixed pork offal including intestines, stomach, and liver. The dish is cooked over high heat with garlic, chilli, and fish sauce, producing a pungent, sour, and savoury flavour profile with a tender-chewy texture from the offal and a tangy crunch from the pickled cabbage. Nutritionally, it is high in protein (19.1 g/100 g) and moderate in fat (10.5 g/100 g), with negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g confirmed by HPB). The dominant clinical concern is the very high sodium content of 1050 mg per 100 g, contributed by both the inherently salty pickled cabbage and the fish sauce seasoning. Cholesterol is elevated at 89 mg/100 g due to the pork organ content. Dietary fibre and potassium are negligible, providing no meaningful cardiovascular offset. This dish is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is typically served as a side dish or main dish accompaniment with steamed rice at lunch or dinner in hawker centres across Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (cr_id G00010535) with a reported confidence of 0.95. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singaporean dishes. Multilingual names are derived from standard Vietnamese, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi translations of the dish components.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g by HPB — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Food Composition Database. The HPB source explicitly reports 0.0 g carbohydrate and 0.0 g sugar for this dish; this reflects the dominance of protein and fat in pork offal with minimal residual carbohydrate from pickled cabbage. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 g per 100 g — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Food Composition Database. Sugar_g is 0.0 g per 100 g — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Food Composition Database. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g per HPB); the numeric value 0 is used as a template-required numeric sentinel and does not represent a measured GI value. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite dish not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry as of 2026. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB entry for this dish. USDA FoodData Central was consulted for supplementary fatty acid and vitamin data but no sufficiently close composite match was identified to justify substitution without introducing estimation error.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. Primary fix in iteration 3: glycemic_index changed from null to numeric 0 per validator feedback (q4 required numeric value). The value is documented as a template-required numeric sentinel for a negligible-carbohydrate food, not a measured GI. Nutrient data sourced exactly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (cr_id G00010535). Zero carbohydrate, zero sugar, and zero dietary fibre documented as confirmed zero per HPB. All -1 fields documented in notes. Multilingual naming satisfies Singaporean dish requirements (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) plus Vietnamese as the dish origin language. All three mandatory health conditions present with correct Type 2 Diabetes human condition name. Ontology anchors are approximate but defensible with gap notes. Aliases are consistent between flat array and structured object.
